comments.author: @graemelee5701
comments.text: In New Zealand, just down the road from the Hobbiton Movie set is the Amazing Matamata Districts Book Club Library. There is an amazing range of over 100,000 quality books available for you to read or take away. NZ and overseas visitors are welcomed, entry if free.  It's well worth visiting. We might even see you there. Google Amazing Matamata Districts Book Club Library 🎉🎉🎉🎉🎉🎉🎉🎉🎉🎉🎉

comments.author: @robomaka2291
comments.text: Sometimes these types of travel videos can miss something so important in the New Zealand landscape and history. The Waipoua Forest on the west coast of Northland contains one of the last vestiges of the mighty Kauri trees that dominated the northern landscape. These huge trees, some 1200 years old, are spectacular and to the visitor provide an almost spiritual engagement.  Similar is the Pureora Forest which in1946 was one of the last native forests to be opened up for logging.  Pureora Forest Park is a 760-square-kilometre (290 sq mi) protected area in the North Island. Within its rich rainforest are an abundance of 1,000-year-old podocarp trees - excellent stands of Rimu, Tawa, Kahikatea, Miro, Matia and Totara. The park provides stunning walks which venture through this remanent of the North Island temperate rainforest.
